title: Structural Exhaustion CT1–CT17 semantic tactic specifications
numberingConvention: CT1-v1, CT2-v2, and CT3-v1 through CT17-v1 use stable semantic node and edge IDs. Numeric fields are one-based presentation order.

signature.inputDescription: Validated Input(F): an ambient object, branch state, and baseline proof. Static target and test vocabulary, exact consumer frameworks for CT2, CT3, CT4, CT5, CT6, and CT17, and their alignment predicates belong to Framework.
signature.parameterDescription: Supply four proof-producing node plans—scope, equivalence, realization, and payload—plus Port and HandoffPlan independently.

mechanism.execution: Execute scope admission, certify target-test equivalence, decide realization, and classify one target-avoiding state into a typed payload. Equivalence failure is a pre-admission repair obligation, not a runtime branch.
mechanism.routing: A realized test closes at C1. Otherwise one indexed payload reaches CT2, CT3, CT4, CT5, CT6, or CT17. Every route carries the actual consumer input and an explicit alignment proof.
mechanism.limit: run_total is relative to the supplied node plans. Target avoidance is available only through EquivalenceCertificate and the negative realization constructor.

statement.informal: Admit the ambient object, branch state, and baseline proof without mixing in node implementations.

obligation.dischargeCriteria: Entry.run preserves the validated Input definitionally.

source.section: CT1 — Target-test alignment and first closure

statement.informal: Decide between a typed scope exit and a scope-ready state.

obligation.dischargeCriteria: The Lean contract resolves and its constructors match the outgoing typed edges exactly.

statement.informal: The semantic run ends at the exact scope outcome.

obligation.dischargeCriteria: The terminal constructor, raw outcome, certified outcome, and incoming typed edge all resolve.

scopeAudit.requiredFinding: Retain the proof that ScopeReadyAt is false and refer the expressibility limit to the global scope audit.

statement.informal: Certify both directions between target membership and realization of the declared test family.

obligation.dischargeCriteria: The plan constructs an EquivalenceState before realization is inspected.

source.section: CT1 — Target-test alignment and first closure

statement.informal: Return either a C1 certificate or a complete target-avoiding state.

obligation.dischargeCriteria: The Lean contract resolves and its constructors match the outgoing typed edges exactly.

statement.informal: The semantic run ends at the exact c1 outcome.

obligation.dischargeCriteria: The terminal constructor, raw outcome, certified outcome, and incoming typed edge all resolve.

certificate.validationStatement: The realized test and certified forward equivalence implication establish the exact target proposition.

statement.informal: Classify one target-avoiding state into exactly one of the six typed consumer payloads.

obligation.dischargeCriteria: The Lean contract resolves and its constructors match the outgoing typed edges exactly.

completeness.numberingPolicy: nodeNumber and transitionNumber are presentation order; semantic IDs are stable runtime identities

totality.statement: For every validated input, CorePlan, Port, and HandoffPlan, run_total produces one indexed outcome and one evidence-carrying path. This does not assert automatic witness search.

source.section: CT1 — Target-test alignment and first closure

signature.inputDescription: Validated Input(F): a minimal counterexample and a proper admissible piece. Interface boundedness is the first decision, not an entry hypothesis.
signature.parameterDescription: Supply CorePlan as five proof-producing decision plans, plus Port and HandoffPlan independently. Runtime totality is relative to these supplied plans.

mechanism.execution: Execute interface admission, deletion search, smaller-replacement-candidate search, candidate-specific context certification, and—only after exhaustive candidate absence—survivor classification. Each decision returns a proof-carrying constructor for exactly one edge.
mechanism.routing: Deletion and certified replacement close in distinct C2 terminals. Interface failure produces scope. A separating context and a survivor response defect are distinct CT3 terminals; a survivor with criticality-refinement data is the CT10 terminal.
mechanism.limit: CT2 does not claim search completeness. run_total is relative to supplied proof-producing plans. Replacement closure requires a certificate for the selected candidate, and raw routed payloads gain consumer-acceptance proofs only through HandoffPlan.

totality.statement: For every validated input, CorePlan, Port, and HandoffPlan, run_total produces one indexed terminal outcome and an evidence-carrying Graph.Path. This is relative totality, not witness-search completeness.

signature.inputDescription: A validated boundaried piece, certified interface, ambient object, and branch state.
signature.parameterDescription: Five independent proof-producing plans for scope, equivalence, compression, defect routing, and finite-table classification, plus a separate consumer port.

mechanism.execution: Admit the finite interface, certify response equivalence, exhaust smaller replacements, classify a concrete defect, and classify the persistent finite table.
mechanism.routing: C2, C3, and C5 are closure terminals. Indexed payloads route exchange data to CT7, measured load to CT12, and repeated types to CT8.
mechanism.limit: A finite type index and response-equivalence certificate are preconditions for comparison; totality is relative to the five supplied node plans.

signature.inputDescription: A validated demand--payer ledger with an explicit partial assignment, capacity datum, and lower-bound datum.
signature.parameterDescription: Five independent proof-producing plans for scope, canonical assignment, payer availability, fibre capacity, and comparison, plus a separate consumer port.

mechanism.execution: Admit the demand family, certify the canonical map, prove totality or exhibit a missing payer, prove bounded fibres or exhibit overload, and compare demand with capacity.
mechanism.routing: C4 is the closure terminal. Indexed payloads route missing payer data to CT13, overload to CT9, and aggregate residual mass to CT14.
mechanism.limit: Canonicality is a certification boundary and cannot be replaced by an unchecked tie-break; totality is relative to the five supplied node plans.

signature.inputDescription: A validated ambient object, branch state, site family, and dependent local-witness extractor.
signature.parameterDescription: Five independent proof-producing plans for scope, locality, deficit routing, summation, and comparison, plus a separate consumer port.

mechanism.execution: Admit bounded witnesses, certify locality, isolate an additive deficit or local ledger, certify the sum, and classify the global comparison.
mechanism.routing: C4 is the closure terminal. Indexed payloads route an exact ledger to CT4, an additive deficit to CT11, and aggregate residual mass to CT14.
mechanism.limit: Locality and summation are certification boundaries; the CT4 adapter preserves the ambient object and branch state definitionally.

signature.inputDescription: A validated dormant-structure datum, ambient object, branch state, and activity ledger.
signature.parameterDescription: Five independent proof-producing plans for scope, the active/dormant definition, activity classification, active-ledger certification, and dormant routing, plus a separate consumer port.

mechanism.execution: Admit a finite activity description, certify its definition, distinguish active from dormant structure, certify the active ledger, and classify dormant residual data.
mechanism.routing: C1 is the closure terminal. Indexed payloads route active demand to CT4 and dormant data to CT3, CT7, CT9, or CT10.
mechanism.limit: The activity predicate and ledger are certification boundaries; totality is relative to the five supplied node plans.

signature.inputDescription: A validated exchange site, two local realizations, ambient object, and branch state.
signature.parameterDescription: Six independent proof-producing plans for scope, exchange-context certification, distinction, realization, defect routing, and neutral routing, plus a separate consumer port.

mechanism.execution: Admit the exchange site, certify its common context, distinguish the two realizations, test realization, classify a defect when present, and classify neutral data otherwise.
mechanism.routing: C1, C2, and C3 are closure terminals. Indexed payloads route context data to CT3, peelable load to CT12, finite labels to CT10, and whole-object data to CT16.
mechanism.limit: Exchange context and realization are explicit proof boundaries; totality is relative to the six supplied node plans.

signature.inputDescription: A validated overloaded payer fibre, capacity datum, ambient object, and branch state.
signature.parameterDescription: Five independent proof-producing plans for scope, fibre certification, overload classification, witness extraction, and routing, plus a separate consumer port.

mechanism.execution: Admit a finite fibre, certify it, separate bounded from overloaded fibres, extract a repeated witness, and classify that witness.
mechanism.routing: C1 is the closure terminal. Indexed payloads route bounded demand to CT4, exchange data to CT7, repetition to CT8, and a finite-description gap to CT10.
mechanism.limit: The fibre and extraction certificates must retain their source ledger; totality is relative to the five supplied node plans.

signature.inputDescription: A validated branch state, routed-load account, peel move, and natural-valued load.
signature.parameterDescription: Six proof-producing plans for scope, initial measure, saturation, canonical peeling, restoration, and strict decrease, plus a separate consumer port.

mechanism.execution: Certify the initial invariant, decide saturation, peel one unit, restore the state, certify strict decrease, and recurse on the smaller load.
mechanism.routing: The unsaturated state closes by C4. Restoration demand routes by exact inputs to CT4 or CT13.
mechanism.limit: Only DecreasedState licenses the back edge; restoration without strict decrease cannot recurse.

signature.inputDescription: A validated tier account, availability datum, measurable resource, and branch state.
signature.parameterDescription: Eight proof-producing plans separating scope, availability, tier-one payment and routing, fallback, reconciliation, comparison, and overlap routing.

mechanism.execution: Decide tier-one availability, certify the selected payment scheme, reconcile payer resources, and close or classify a named overlap.

mechanism.limit: Fallback requires a minimal canonical obstruction, and overlap routing requires an explicit failed reconciliation state.

signature.parameterDescription: Four proof-producing plans for support, whole-object scope, closed-type certification, and literal equality.

mechanism.execution: Route proper support immediately; for whole support, certify an exact closed type and decide literal equality.

mechanism.limit: Finite closed-type scope is required only after the support decision proves that the datum is whole-object.

signature.parameterDescription: Seven proof-producing plans for scope, compatibility, separation, block certification, scale, finite survivors, and arithmetic.
